DISCOVERY 302, CROSS COMPLAINANTS JUN TAN AND LINDA HO'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON MOTION TO COMPEL CORPORATE PARTY TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION, TO EXTEND DISCOVERY CUTOFF DATE, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. CORPORATE BOOKS AND RECORDS ARE RELEVANT TO THE ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS. MOREOVER, THEY ARE NOT PROTECTED UNDER THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE OR ATTORNEY-WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGE. THE CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTION DOES NOT PROTECT A CORPORATION'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY TO ITS FINANCIAL RECORDS. THE COURT DIRECTS THE PARTIES TO SUBMIT PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDERS. THE INSURANCE POLICY ALSO FALLS UNDER THE SCOPE OF AUTHORIZED DISCOVERY. THE COURT GRANTS A 60 DAY EXTENSION OF THE DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE. MR. CUR SHOULD BE QUESTIONED FOR NO MORE THAN ONE HOUR. THE COURT AWARDS SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFF COUNSEL, AMOUNT OF SANCTIONS IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, NOT REPORTED. ==================================================================== AFTER HEARING, ON PLAINTIFF'S SANCTIONS REQUEST, THE COURT GRANTS SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000.00; DEFENDANTS AND DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL MUST EACH REMIT $1,000.00 TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BY JULY 31, 2011.

CROSS-COMPLAINANTS' ALLEGATION THAT THE ACTUAL CONSTRUCTION WORK WAS DONE BY PLAINTIFF, WHO IS NOT A LICENSED CONTRACTOR, STRONGLY SUGGESTS THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE NOT OWED ANY MONEY FOR THE WORK AND ARE SEEKING TO COLLECT FOR WORK PERFORMED BY A PERSON WHO IS LEGALLY UNABLE TO COLLECT FOR CONSTRUCTION WORK. THE FURTHER ALLEGATION THAT PLAINTIFF'S "STATUS IN THE EYES OF THE LAW WAS THAT AS AN EMPLOYEE OF" THE INDIVIDUAL CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS BOTH A CONCLUSION NOT BASED ON ANY ULTIMATE FACTS AND INCOMPREHENSIBLE WITHOUT SOME EXPLANATION AS TO WHY PLAINTIFF IS OR SHOULD BE DEEMED AN EMPLOYEE OF THE INDIVIDUAL CROSS-COMPLAINANT. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE GRANTED LEAVE, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS WHY THEY ARE OWED MONEY FOR THE CONSTRUCTION WORK PERFORMED BY PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
CROSS-DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS JUN TAN AND LINDA HO'S DEMURRER OR MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

APPEARANCES: Steven W. Ferla, Esq. of Menekshe Law Firm 408/358-1200 for opposing parties Kevin Curran and Curran & Associates, Inc.; George W. Wolff, Esq. of Law Office of George William Wolff 788-1881 for moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is off calendar as moot. Cross-complainants' allegation that the actual construction work was done by plaintiff, who is not a licensed contractor, strongly suggests that cross-complainants are not owed any money for the work and are seeking to collect for work performed by a person who is legally unable to collect for construction work. The further allegation that plaintiff's "status in the eyes of the law was that as an employee of" the individual cross-complainant is both a conclusion not based on any ultimate facts and incomprehensible without some explanation as to why plaintiff is or should be deemed an employee of the individual cross-complainant. Cross-complainants are granted leave, if they can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts why they are owed money for the construction work performed by plaintiff. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows.
CROSS COMPLAINANTS JUN TAN AND LINDA HO'S Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Compel Corporate Party To Produce Documents At Deposition, To Extend Discovery Cutoff Date, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Cross-defendant.
Defendant/cross complainants' motion to compel production of documents is granted.
Corporate books and records are relevant to the alter ego allegations. Moreover, they are not protected under the attorney-client privilege or attorney-work product privilege. The California Constitution does not protect a corporation's right to privacy to its financial records. The court directs the parties to submit proposed protective orders.
The insurance policy also falls under the scope of authorized discovery. The court grants a 60 day extension of the discovery cut-off date.
Mr. Cur should be questioned for no more than one hour.
The court awards sanctions to plaintiff Counsel, amount of sanctions is taken under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Not Reported.
